Qualcomm Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.89 a Share, Payable March 26 to Holders of Record as of March 5
State Street: profit slips despite rising revenue
data.description:
State Street said on Friday that its net profit fell 5% in Q4 2025, weighed on by a sharp rise in expenses, including restructuring costs relating to job cuts and investments in technology. Net profit came in at $747m, or $2.42 per share, compared with $783m ($2.46 per share) in the same period a year earlier, despite broad-based revenue growth.

Against a supportive market backdrop marked by the rise of artificial intelligence and expectations of lower interest rates, State Street's assets under custody and administration rose 16% to $53.8 trillion. Fee-related revenue, the group's main growth driver, jumped 8% to $2.86bn, helped in particular by a 15% increase in management fees and an 8% rise in servicing fees. Net interest income also topped expectations, coming in at $802m.

However, total expenses rose 12.3% y-o-y to $2.74bn. This increase includes $206m in one-off charges, directly weighing on the group's profitability. In response, State Street shares were down nearly 3% ahead of the opening bell.

data.title: Central Pattana Public Company Limited Announces Executive Changes, Effective May 1, 2026
data.description: Central Pattana Public Company Limited announced that the Board of Directors meeting held on January 16, 2026, the Board has made a resolution concurring with the proposal made by the Nomination and Remuneration Committee to appoint Mr. Chanavat Uahwatanasakul as the President of the Company, in replacement of Miss Wallaya Chirathivat who will retire from such position, effective on May 1, 2026 onwards. However, Miss Wallaya Chirathivat still holds directorship in the Company.

data.title: Central Pattana Public Company Limited Announces Chief Executive Officer Changes, Effective May 1, 2026
data.description: Central Pattana Public Company Limited Board of Directors meeting held on January 16, 2026, the Board has made a resolution concurring with the proposal made by the Nomination and Remuneration Committee to appoint Mr. Chanavat Uahwatanasakul as the Chief Executive Officer of the Company, in replacement of Miss Wallaya Chirathivat who will retire from such position, effective on May 1, 2026 onwards. However, Miss Wallaya Chirathivat still holds directorship in the Company.

data.title: Kalyan Jewellers India Limited Announces Cessation of Independent Director Kishori J Udeshi Upon Completion of Tenure, Effective January 16, 2026
data.description:
Kalyan Jewellers India Limited announced that Ms. Kishori J Udeshi, Non-Executive Independent Director of the Company, has completed her second consecutive term of appointment and accordingly ceased to hold office as an Independent Director of the Company upon the close of business hours on January 16, 2026. The cessation was communicated in compliance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. Ms. Kishori J Udeshi completed her second and final term as an Independent Director, marking the end of her tenure.

The cessation and disclosure process reflects the company's commitment to corporate governance practices, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards.

Indian Railway Catering And Tourism Corporation Limited Announces Cessation Of Shri Manoj Kumar Gangeya as Executive Director and Part-time Government Director, Effective January 16, 2026
data.description: Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation Limited has announced that Shri Manoj Kumar Gangeya has relinquished the charge of the post of Executive Director (Planning), Railway Board, with effect from January 16, 2026, and accordingly ceases to be the Part-time Government Director on the Board of the Company with effect from January 16, 2026. The cessation of directorship follows the Railway Board's Order No. 2026/E(O)II/5/2 dated January 12, 2026.

Sector Update: Financial
Reliance Industries Reports Higher Net Profit
data.description:
By Nina Kienle


Reliance Industries reported a rise in third-quarter net profit from a year earlier due to an increase in its oil-to-chemicals segment.

The Indian conglomerate said Friday that net profit rose to 186.45 billion Indian rupees, equivalent to $2.06 billion, for the three months ended December, from 185.40 billion rupees in the prior year's period.

That missed the estimate of 196.44 billion rupees in a poll of analysts by data provider LSEG.

Revenue increased to 2.695 trillion rupees from 2.439 trillion rupees.

Earnings before interest and taxes for its oil-to-chemicals segment climbed to 139.21 billion rupees from 128.19 billion rupees.


Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-16-26 0912ET

Johnson & Johnson Announces New Analysis of Phase 3 Data Which Found Caplyta®? (Lumateperone) in Combination with an Antibody
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson announced a new analysis of Phase 3 data which found CAPLYTA®? (lumateperone), in combination with an antidepressant, showed significantly greater remission rates in adults with major depressive disorder (MDD) than placebo plus an antidepressant at six weeks, with continued benefits observed through six months in an open-label extension study. The findings are featured in one of 11 abstracts from across Johnson & Johnson's neuropsychiatry portfolio that were presented at the 64th Annual Meeting of the American College of Neuropsychopharmacology (ACNP), held from January 12-15, inassau, Bahamas.

Detailed study findings: In the pooled pivotal data, almost twice as many patients reached remission (MADRS 10) at six weeks with adjunctive CAPLYTA®? compared to placebo (25.5% versus 13.6%; nominal p2A receptor occupancy and moderate amounts of dopamine D2 receptor occupancy at therapeutic doses. CAPLYTA®?

does not need dose titration, allowing patients to start treatment at the effective dose of 42 mg.3,10 A supplemental New Drug Application (sNDA) for CAPLYTA®? with long-term data evaluating the safety and efficacy of the medication for the prevention of relapse in schizophrenia was submitted to the FDA. The medication is also being studied for other neuropsychiatric disorders.

CAPLYTA®? is not FDA-approved for these disorders. This press release is not sanctioned by ACNP.

Importantly, metabolic and weight changes were similar to placebo and the rates of extrapyramidal symptoms were low. Study 503 is a 26-week, open-label extension study that investigated the long-term safety of adjunctive CAPLYTA?? 42mg in patients who completed Study 501 or 502.

The primary endpoint was safety and tolerability of CAPLYTA®?, measured by adverse events (AEs), extrapyramidal symptoms (EPS), suicidality, and changes in laboratory parameters, vital signs, and electrocardiogram (ECG) measures. CAPLYTA is available in 42 mg, 21 mg, and 10.5 mg capsules. If underlying assumptions prove inaccurate or known or unknown risks or uncertainties materialize, actual results could vary materially from the expectations and projections of Johnson & Johnson.

R risks and uncertainties include, but are not limited to: challenges and uncertainties inherent in product research and development, including the uncertainty of clinical success and of obtaining regulatory approvals; uncertainty of commercial success; manufacturing difficulties and delays; competition, including technological advances, new products and patents attained by competitors; challenges to patents; product efficacy or safety concerns resulting in product recalls or regulatory action; changes in behavior and spending patterns of purchasers of health care products and services; changes to applicable laws and regulations, including global health care reforms; and trends toward health care cost containment. A further list and descriptions of these risks, uncertainties and other factors can be found in Johnson & Johnson's most recent Annual Report on Form 10.

Trump to Push for Tech Giants to Pay for Power Costs
data.description:
President Donald Trump, along with the governors of several US Northeastern states, agreed to push for an emergency wholesale electricity auction that would effectively force technology companies to fund new power plants. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television.

data.title: Dealmakers see more retail mergers and IPOs in 2026 after tariffs sidelined M&A last year
Canada Housing Starts Rise 11% in December, Though Trend Remains Soft
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart


OTTAWA--Canadian housing starts ended last year on a stronger note, recovering from recent softness as housing activity has been held back by uncertainty in the wake of the Trump administration's shift in trade policy and embrace of tariffs.

Housing starts across Canada came in at a seasonally adjusted annualized rate of 282,439 units in December, an 11% rise from the month before, Canada Mortgage and Housing Corp. said Friday. The market was expecting about 260,000 residential housing projects to have started in December, according to economists at TD Securities.

Still, the trend measure--a six-month moving average of the monthly seasonally adjusted annual rate of housing starts--slipped 0.1% to 264,428 units last month, Canada's national housing agency said.

Actual housing starts increased 25% from a year earlier in centers with a population of 10,000 or greater, with 20,716 units recorded in December to mark the most starts for that month on record, largely driven by increases in Ontario.

For the full year, housing starts in all areas in Canada hit 259,028, the fifth highest annual total on record, and up 5.6% from the year before. Actual starts in centers with a 10,000-plus population were up 6% for all of 2025, with 241,171 units recorded.

"While housing starts in 2025 finished ahead of 2024 and inched up in December, most of the momentum in housing construction occurred in the spring and summer. Since September, the trend in housing starts has consistently decreased," said Mathieu Laberge, chief economist at the agency.

Laberge said housing starts begin the new year from a weaker position. "Market intelligence suggests slowing momentum for residential construction. These trends, along with geopolitical and trade uncertainty, remain top of mind."

Canada's six largest metropolitan Areas saw a combined 3.9% year-over-year increase in starts last year, driven by record annual starts in Calgary and Edmonton, Alberta, a 58% annual increase in Montreal, and a 12% increase in the Ottawa-Gatineau region. Moderating that, Toronto starts for the year fell 31% and Vancouver declined about 3%, the agency said.

Broadly, Canada's housing market finished 2025 on a soft note after what was a choppy year that began with expectations of a pickup in activity until the trade dispute with the U.S. and its tariffs hit consumer and business sentiment.

After a mid-year jump in sales, sales activity pulled back more recently, with sales of existing homes and prices declined in December. There was a sharp drop in building permits for residential housing construction in November, the latest month available, with the value falling 12% from a month earlier.


Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-16-26 0857ET

data.title: Reliance Industries Q3 Jio ARPU 213.7 rupees per subscriber/month
Sector Update: Tech
Billionaire Mukesh Ambani's Reliance misses quarterly profit view
data.description:
Jan 16 (Reuters) - Indian billionaire Mukesh Ambani's Reliance Industries missed quarterly profit estimates on Friday, hurt by drop in revenue at its key oil and gas segment.

Consolidated profit came in at 186.45 billion rupees ($2.06 billion) for the quarter ending December 31, missing analysts' average estimate of 196.44 billion rupees, according to data compiled by LSEG.

Revenue from its oil and gas segment fell 8.4%, making it the only segment to report a decline.

Reliance, operator of the world's largest refining complex, said on January 6 it was not expecting any Russian crude oil deliveries, a move that could sharply cut India's Russian oil imports during the month to the lowest in years.

($1 = 90.7260 Indian rupees)

(Reporting by Chandini Monnappa in Bengaluru; Editing by Mrigank Dhaniwala)
